task_completion.description: Select dive #4 from the dive list
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dive list panel shows dive #4 highlighted or selected.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the equipment or cylinder settings for the selected dive
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Equipment', 'Cylinders', or similar tab is active in the dive details panel, displaying cylinder information.

task_completion.description: Set the start pressure of the first cylinder to 207 bar
task_completion.visual_evidence: The start pressure input field for the first cylinder contains the value '207' (or '207 bar').

task_completion.description: Set the end pressure of the first cylinder to 65 bar
task_completion.visual_evidence: The end pressure input field for the first cylinder contains the value '65' (or '65 bar').

task_completion.description: Save the changes to the dive log file
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent clicks a 'Save' button, selects 'File -> Save' from the menu, or presses a save shortcut. The application title bar may also lose its unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk).

integrity.description: Agent used the application GUI to make changes
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Subsurface (or dive log) graphical user interface, rather than terminal commands modifying the file.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a text editor to bypass the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The file is not being edited in a text editor like nano, vim, gedit, or VS Code.

integrity.description: Agent did not use automated scripts to modify the file
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal windows do not show Python, bash, or XML parsing scripts (like xmlstarlet) being executed to alter the .ssrf file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target dive number is 4. The expected start pressure is 207 bar and the expected end pressure is 65 bar. The log file being edited is located at /home/ga/Documents/dives.ssrf.
